meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registration #188008), uplead.com/privacy, uplead.com/opt-out-request. Fined by CPPA for late registration. Headquartered at 340 S Lemon Ave Ste 1022, Walnut, CA 91789.

scan.reason: Cloud software company for life sciences and healthcare. Maintains databases of healthcare professionals and their prescribing data. No public consumer search.

optout.notes: Cloud platform for life sciences. Maintains healthcare professional databases (Veeva Network/OpenData). To opt out, select your relationship to Veeva (e.g., Healthcare Professional or Scientific Expert). Contact Chief Privacy Officer at privacy@veeva.com or call toll-free 1-877-807-3230.

meta.notes: Source: Veeva privacy policy, Do Not Sell/Share page. Veeva does not claim to sell/share sensitive personal information. Healthcare professionals are the primary data subjects. GDPR compliant for EU data.

scan.reason: B2B location data provider. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to Veraset Do Not Sell My Info page
Submit the Do Not Sell request

optout.notes: Requires mobile Advertising ID for opt-out. Veraset provides raw GPS data to academic researchers, hedge funds, and government agencies.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registered as Veraset). Provides raw GPS location data derived from mobile devices. Clients include academic researchers, hedge funds, real estate firms, and government agencies. Data collected via SDK partners embedded in mobile apps.

steps.description: Go to Veripages privacy control page

optout.notes: Can also email support@veripages.com. Phone: (844) 217-0978. Click 'Control Profile' on any listing to opt out.

meta.notes: Related to Vericora network of sites. Source: State of Surveillance Master List, Security.org opt-out guide.

scan.reason: B2B data analytics and marketing solutions provider. No consumer-facing search. Consumer opt-out via Infutor privacy portal.

optout.notes: Verisk acquired Infutor Data Solutions. Consumer opt-out portal at privacy.infutor.com. Identity verification may be required. Removal within 45 days per CCPA.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, Verisk/Infutor privacy portal. Verisk acquired Infutor Data Solutions. State data privacy notice covers CA, CO, CT, NJ, UT, VA and others.

scan.reason: Insurance claims database and analytics. No public consumer search. Consumers can request their own A-PLUS Property or auto claims report.

methods.notes: Request your Verisk consumer report (A-PLUS Property, ISO ClaimSearch). FCRA-regulated.

optout.notes: Verisk ISO ClaimSearch is a comprehensive claims database used by insurers. A-PLUS Property reports track property insurance claims history. FCRA-regulated for insurance reporting. Consumer reports can be requested via fcra.verisk.com. Separate from Verisk/Infutor marketing data entry. Privacy requests to privacy@verisk.com.

meta.notes: Source: CFPB consumer reporting companies list (A-PLUS Property by Verisk), Verisk privacy policies, fcra.verisk.com. Separate from the existing Verisk/Infutor marketing data broker entry. Focuses on insurance claims and loss history data.

scan.reason: B2B programmatic advertising and location data platform. No consumer-facing search. Provides location-based audience targeting for mobile and CTV advertising.

methods.notes: Toll-free number. Use Verve Service Code: 2178#.

optout.notes: Verve Group provides location-based mobile and connected TV (CTV) advertising. Uses location data associated with device identifiers for ad targeting and audience segmentation. Identity verification required before processing requests.

meta.notes: Source: verve.com/product-privacy-policies. Headquartered at 60 Madison Avenue, 8th Floor, New York, NY 10010. Programmatic advertising platform specializing in location-based mobile and CTV targeting.

steps.description: Find your individual record page on VoterRecords
Scroll to bottom of record page and click 'Record Opt-Out' link
Click verification link in email from VoterRecords

optout.notes: Partial removal only: house number, phone, and email hidden. Full record removal only for individuals with official public records exemption. Covers 18 states plus DC.

meta.notes: Only publishes voter data for 18 states: AK, AR, CO, CT, DE, FL, ID, LA, MI, MS, NV, NJ, NC, OH, OK, RI, UT, WA plus DC. Source: Optery, Incogni, Onerep.

scan.reason: B2B email hygiene and data enrichment platform. No consumer-facing search. Provides email verification, data appending, and audience targeting.

steps.description: Go to Webbula opt-out request form

optout.notes: Webbula provides email verification, data appending, and audience targeting services. Fulfilled all 8,516 opt-out requests received in 2023 with 0 denied. Alternative phone: 800-533-5853.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registration #186746), webbula.com/privacy-center, webbula.com/our-ccpa-commitment. Headquartered at 5000 Stonewood Drive, Suite 310, Wexford, PA 15090. Email hygiene and data enrichment provider.

steps.description: Go to Whitepages suppression request page
Enter the URL of your listing
Enter phone number for verification call
Receive automated verification call with code

optout.notes: Requires phone verification call. May need to remove from multiple listings.

meta.notes: Phone verification makes automation harder. CAPTCHA present.

scan.reason: B2B location data broker. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to X-Mode CCPA opt-out / Do Not Sell form
Enter your Device ID (Advertising ID or Installation ID)

optout.notes: FTC ordered X-Mode/Outlogic to stop selling sensitive location data (April 2024). Company sold data that could track visits to abortion clinics, places of worship, domestic violence shelters. Failed to honor Android opt-out requests. Requires Device ID (MAID or Installation ID) for opt-out. Sells data to government contractors and law enforcement.

meta.notes: Source: outlogic.io/privacy-policy, FTC enforcement action (Jan 2024, finalized Apr 2024). FTC prohibited from selling sensitive location data. Sold location data to hundreds of clients including government contractors and law enforcement. Collected data via own apps and third-party SDK partners. Rebranded from X-Mode Social to Outlogic.

steps.description: Scroll to bottom and click 'Suppress My Data' link
You will be redirected to PeopleConnect suppression center
Verify email via link from PeopleConnect (15 min expiry)
Select your record and set to Suppressed

optout.notes: Owned by Intelius/PeopleConnect. Redirects to PeopleConnect Suppression Center. Single suppression covers all PeopleConnect brands.

meta.notes: Subsidiary of Intelius/PeopleConnect. Suppression propagates across all PeopleConnect sites. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, IntelTechniques Workbook

scan.reason: B2B customer data platform and identity resolution provider. No consumer-facing search. Provides CDP, identity resolution, and data enrichment services.

steps.description: Go to Zeotap privacy opt-out page
Opt out of personal information stored against cookie IDs

methods.notes: Include your Ad ID if requesting mobile advertising opt-out

optout.notes: Zeotap provides a customer data platform (CDP) with identity resolution capabilities. Also operates 'Zeotap Fuel' for third-party identity resolution. Supports Right to Erasure / Right to Be Forgotten via Delete API. For mobile ad ID opt-out, include your Ad ID in the email request.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registration #186755), zeotap.com/privacy-policy, PrivacyHawk opt-out guide. German-headquartered CDP and identity resolution company. Registered as zeotap GmbH in California.

scan.reason: B2B marketing technology platform. No consumer-facing search. Provides identity-based marketing data, email marketing, and programmatic advertising.

steps.description: Go to Zeta Global rights request page (for programmatic/pseudonymous data)
Go to Zeta Global OneTrust privacy portal (for deterministic/identified data)
Submit opt-out request for identified data

optout.notes: Zeta Global maintains two types of data: deterministic (fully identifiable PII) and programmatic (pseudonymous cookie/device data). Separate opt-out forms exist for each type. Submit to both for complete removal. Claims 235M+ US adult identity records.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registration #192108), zetaglobal.com/how-zeta-protects-privacy-and-manages-data-responsibly. Headquartered at 3 Park Ave, 33rd Floor, New York, NY 10016. Claims data on 235M+ US adults. Two separate opt-out paths for deterministic vs. programmatic data.

scan.reason: Public property search available. Address lookup shows property details, estimated values, tax history, and ownership information.

methods.notes: Zillow Group Privacy Portal for exercising privacy rights.
Zillow Customer Care Team. Use for multiple property opt-outs or extenuating circumstances.

optout.notes: Largest online real estate marketplace. Displays property details, Zestimates (home value estimates), tax history, and ownership for virtually every US property. Also operates Trulia, HotPads, and StreetEasy. Contact privacy@zillow.com or use the Zillow Group Privacy Portal.

meta.notes: Source: Zillow Group privacy policy, Zillow Group Privacy Portal. Property data sourced from public records and is difficult to fully remove. BBB Digital Advertising Accountability Program found compliance issues in Dec 2025.

scan.reason: B2B sales intelligence platform. Requires paid subscription to search.

steps.description: Go to ZoomInfo Privacy Center removal page
Click 'Request Removal' to receive verification code
Check email for 6-digit verification code from ZoomInfo

methods.notes: ZoomInfo privacy request phone line

optout.notes: Requires email verification with 6-digit code. Full name is optional on removal form. Profile removed from website and subscription products. Opt-out shared with ZoomInfo customers. Heavily scrapes LinkedIn.

meta.notes: Source: privacyrequest.zoominfo.com, zoominfo.com/trust-center/your-privacy, California Data Broker Registry. Largest B2B contact database. Scrapes LinkedIn, company websites, SEC filings, job boards. Claims GDPR and CCPA compliance.

scan.reason: Political data analytics company. No public consumer search. Maintains database of 250+ million adults including 190 million registered voters.

methods.notes: Consumer request portal accessible from i360 privacy policy page.
i360 CCPA information line.

optout.notes: Koch-funded political data analytics company. Maintains database of 250+ million adults. Knows demographics, ethnicity, religion, occupation, hobbies, shopping habits, financial assets, health conditions, and more. Products used by NRA, Republican party committees, and conservative political organizations. Opt-out via consumer request portal or CCPA phone line.

meta.notes: Source: i360 privacy policy, JoinDeleteMe opt-out guide, SourceWatch, PR Watch investigation. Koch brothers' data operation, merged with Themis voter database project. Extensive consumer profiling.
